description: Name of the W&B run shown in the UI
Labels attached to the W&B run for filtering/grouping
Global random seed for reproducibility
Percentiles used for lift@K evaluation metrics
Metric returned to Hydra sweeper for hyperparameter optimization
Path to provider-month CSV produced by run_provider_month.sh
Path to existing provider_level CSV to skip recomputation. null = compute fresh next to provider_month_csv
Minimum months a provider must be observed to be included. Providers below this get insufficient_history_flag=1 and are filtered out
Drop all provider-month rows after this date before building features
If true, keep providers with insufficient history (ignore min_months filter)
Feature columns with more than this fraction of NaN values are dropped. Then rows with any remaining NaN are dropped
Keep only features with univariate AUROC >= this threshold. Set to null to disable feature selection and use all features
Scaling applied to features before training. Options: standardize (zero mean unit variance) | minmax (0-1 range) | none
Number of providers per training batch
Number of DataLoader worker processes for parallel data loading. 0 = main process only
Pin DataLoader tensors to CPU memory for faster GPU transfer. Set true when using CUDA GPU
Minimum prior value for percent-growth features. Pairs where prior < floor are skipped to avoid near-zero blowup
Number of prior observed months used as the rolling window for robust-z anomaly flags
A month is flagged if its robust-z score exceeds this threshold
Count of flagged months is computed over the last k observed months for recency-weighted features
PELT changepoint detection penalty. Higher = fewer changepoints detected
PELT cost model. Options: rbf | l1 | l2 | normal
Minimum number of observations in a segment for PELT
Minimum observations a provider needs for changepoint detection to run
Fraction of providers held out for val/loss monitoring and early stopping. Set 0.0 to disable early stopping and train for full max_epochs
Random seed for val split selection
Hidden layer sizes of the encoder. Decoder mirrors these in reverse. More/larger dims = more capacity
Size of the compressed bottleneck representation. Smaller = more compression = stronger anomaly signal
Dropout probability applied after each encoder/decoder layer. Higher = more regularization
Activation function used in encoder and decoder. Options: relu | leaky_relu | tanh | selu | gelu
Whether to apply batch normalization after each linear layer. Helps training stability
Adam learning rate. Lower = slower but more stable training
L2 regularization penalty on weights. Higher = stronger regularization
Maximum number of training epochs. Training may stop earlier if EarlyStopping triggers
Minimum epochs to train regardless of EarlyStopping
Clip gradients to this max norm. Prevents exploding gradients
Device to train on. auto detects GPU/MPS automatically. Options: auto | cpu | gpu | mps
Run validation every N epochs
Stop training if val/loss does not improve for this many epochs
Number of best checkpoints to keep. -1 = keep all
W&B project name. Runs are grouped under this project in the W&B UI
If true, log locally without syncing to W&B cloud
